params.description: View the diff between last two commits

final_text:
I have all the evidence I need. Here's the analysis:

The bug patch removed two critical lines from the `text` property (lines 924-925 in the original):

```python
if self.encoding is None:
    encoding = self.apparent_encoding
```

**What this means:** When `self.encoding` is `None` (i.e., the HTTP response headers didn't specify a charset), the original code would fall back to `apparent_encoding` — which uses `chardet`/`charset_normalizer` to detect the encoding from the content bytes. With the patch, `encoding` stays `None`, and `str(self.content, None, errors="replace")` raises a `TypeError`, which is caught and falls through to `str(self.content, errors="replace")` — which uses the Python default (UTF-8), ignoring the actual encoding of the content.

```json
[
  {
    "title": "Removal of apparent_encoding fallback causes incorrect text decoding for non-UTF-8 responses",
    "category": "correctness",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 0.99,
    "file": "requests/models.py",
    "start_line": 926,
    "end_line": 930,
    "evidence": [
      "Line 921: encoding = self.encoding  # encoding is None when no charset in HTTP headers",
      "Removed lines: 'if self.encoding is None: encoding = self.apparent_encoding'",
      "Line 930: str(self.content, encoding, errors='replace') — encoding is now None, raises TypeError",
      "Line 938: fallback str(self.content, errors='replace') uses Python default UTF-8, ignoring actual content encoding",
      "Line 791-793: apparent_encoding uses chardet.detect() to determine actual encoding from content bytes"
    ],
    "risk": "Any HTTP response without an explicit charset in Content-Type (very common — many servers omit it) that is encoded in a non-UTF-8 encoding (e.g., ISO-8859-1, Shift_JIS, GB2312, Windows-1252) will be decoded as UTF-8 instead of the correct encoding. This produces mojibake (garbled text) or replacement characters for all non-ASCII content. This silently corrupts data for a huge number of real-world web pages and APIs, especially those serving content in CJK languages, Eastern European languages, or legacy encodings.",
    "suggested_fix": "Restore the removed lines after line 926 ('# Fallback to auto-detected encoding.'):\n\n        if self.encoding is None:\n            encoding = self.apparent_encoding"
  }
]
```
